title: Manager Project Tools (m/f/d)
required_skills: University degree in Business Administration, IT or similar
Strong organisation and project management skills
Experience in project management tools (Jira, Confluence)
Experience in defining project tool standards, guidelines, and templates
Analytical skills and conceptual thinking with cross-process approach
Excellent communication, interpersonal, and presentation skills
Capability to coordinate tool experts and functional users in programme setting
bonus_skills: Can-do attitude, ability to create standards where none exist
matched_skills: JIRA and Confluence: listed as CV tools — hands-on user
Project management standards and documentation: HSBC — PMO governance templates, status reports, process documentation
Programme management organisation skills: HSBC 6-workstream coordination, Hapag-Lloyd cross-functional coordination
Analytical and conceptual thinking: HSBC data storytelling, Hapag-Lloyd research synthesis
Communication and presentation: HSBC C-suite reporting, town hall facilitation
Additional languages: German B1, Chinese C2, Cantonese C2
missing_skills: Technical tool integration experience (BPM, workflow engines, process mining)
PMO tool stack design / technical configuration
core_responsibilities: Define, develop, and improve end-to-end tool stack supporting agile transformation and programme reporting
Design and configure tool-supported processes, templates, and guidance
Implement tools in collaboration with IT partners and business stakeholders
Support technical integrations across tools (Jira, workflow engines, automation platforms)
Coach project teams on agile practices for seamless tool adoption
Support Senior Director in driving programme delivery and project management standards
Collaborate with central PMO on methodology adoption, training, and onboarding
recommended_emphasis: JIRA and Confluence proficiency — directly required tools, present on CV
HSBC PMO governance: templates, status reporting, documentation standards — direct evidence of project tool standards experience
Multi-workstream programme coordination at HSBC as evidence of managing cross-functional tool users
Hapag-Lloyd Confluence/JIRA use for agile sprint delivery documentation
Power BI PL-300 certification as evidence of tool adoption and data visualisation capability
Language skills (4 languages) as strong bonus for global role
summary: Score 75.0 (strong match). This is Boru's strongest match in the set. The role centres on JIRA/Confluence tool ownership, PMO standards, and cross-functional tool coordination — precisely what Boru did at HSBC (programme governance, documentation, templates) and Hapag-Lloyd (agile tooling). The Business Administration degree, JIRA/Confluence experience, and programme coordination background are direct hits. The tool technical integration aspect (BPM, workflow engines) is a gap but the role context is a large PMO transformation programme where the people/standards side is equally valued. Prioritise this application.
